raw_consumer_statement:
XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX charged my American Express credit card on XX/XX/XXXX, without my authorization. I previously attempted to cancel my subscription in XXXX after realizing they had charged me without consent. I called their office, but nobody answered the phone, and I submitted a cancellation form on their website but received no response. I disputed the charge with American Express in XXXX ( see proof of dispute attached ), explaining that I had attempted to cancel but was unable to reach anyone at the company. AMEX denied my dispute. 

At that time, I explicitly asked AMEX to block all future charges from XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX. However, I recently discovered that the same company charged me again in XXXX and again on XX/XX/XXXX. This is the third consecutive year that XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX has charged my account, despite my clear cancellation attempts and my request that AMEX prevent further transactions. 

These ongoing charges are unauthorized. The company has not provided a functional way to cancel the subscription.

This situation is a violation of my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ), which requires credit card issuers to conduct a reasonable investigation into disputed charges. It may also violate the FTC 's rules on automatic renewals, which require companies to clearly disclose terms, obtain express informed consent, and provide an easy way to cancel. XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX failed to do any of this. 

Additionally, AMEX 's failure to block these charges after my XXXX request raises serious concerns. Under CFPB guidelines and general expectations of consumer protection, card issuers must take appropriate steps when a customer flags a merchant for unauthorized recurring transactions. 

I am requesting a full refund of the charges made by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX. That is {$240.00} for each year 3 times = {$740.00}. I also request an investigation into both the merchants behavior and AMEXs handling of my repeated disputes and instructions. 

This ongoing situation is unfair, financially harmful, and completely avoidable had AMEX and XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX followed the law and industry standards.
